title: World Cup Today: Schedule, TV, Streaming Info
body:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup has reached its quarterfinal stage, and today's matches promise to be thrilling encounters that will keep fans on the edge of their seats. The spotlight will be on two high-profile clashes, as Norway takes on England in Miami, while Argentina faces off against Switzerland in Kansas. These matchups bring together some of the biggest names in international football, including Erling Haaland, Harry Kane, and Lionel Messi, ensuring that the excitement will be palpable. With the stakes higher than ever, the world will be watching as these four teams battle it out for a spot in the semifinals.

The first match of the day pits Norway against England, a clash that promises to be a closely contested affair. Norway, led by the prolific Erling Haaland, has been one of the surprise packages of the tournament, with the young striker's goalscoring prowess catching the attention of fans worldwide. England, on the other hand, will be relying on the experience and leadership of Harry Kane to guide them through this tough test. The Three Lions have been solid, if unspectacular, in the tournament so far, but they will need to be at their best to overcome a Norwegian side that has nothing to lose.

In the second match of the day, Argentina will be looking to continue their quest for World Cup glory when they face Switzerland. Lionel Messi, widely regarded as one of the greatest players of all time, has been in scintillating form, inspiring his team to victories with his mesmerizing skill and creativity. Switzerland, however, will not be pushovers, having shown their resilience and determination in the earlier stages of the tournament. They will need to be at their defensive best to contain the Argentine attack, which has been firing on all cylinders.

As the world watches these two quarterfinal matches, the anticipation is building up to a fever pitch. The 2026 FIFA World Cup has already thrown up several surprises, and today's games could potentially spring a few more. The pressure is mounting on the favorites, and the underdogs are sensing their chance to create history. With the tournament reaching its business end, every match counts, and the teams that can handle the pressure will be the ones that ultimately succeed.

For fans looking to catch the action live, the matches will be broadcast on major television networks, with streaming options also available for those who prefer to watch online. The global reach of the World Cup is a testament to its enduring appeal, with people from all corners of the globe tuning in to witness the drama and excitement of the beautiful game. As the quarterfinals unfold, the stage is set for a thrilling conclusion to the tournament, with the best teams and players in the world vying for the ultimate prize.

As the day's matches come to a close, the focus will shift to the next round of fixtures, with the winners of today's games advancing to the semifinals. The 2026 FIFA World Cup has already been a tournament to remember, with its fair share of upsets, comebacks, and individual brilliance. With the stakes getting higher by the day, the world will be watching with bated breath as the remaining teams battle it out for the coveted title. One thing is certain – the next few days will be an unforgettable ride for football fans everywhere, as the beautiful game continues to captivate and inspire us all.
